frameworks.name: Bayesian perception
frameworks.one_line: Priors from perceptual history bias current perception

frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: Paper probes whether the bias acts on encoding or decision

frameworks.name: surround tilt illusion
frameworks.one_line: Used as a tool to dissociate perceived vs physical orientation

core_question: Does serial dependence bias early sensory encoding or only later perceptual decisions?
key_assumptions: Perceptual systems exploit input stability via history-based priors
Serial dependence is maximal when past and present stimuli are similar
Surround flankers shift perceived orientation away from physical orientation
manipulations: Prior stimulus type: illusory (flanked) vs neutral
Current stimulus type: illusory vs neutral
Match basis: physical orientation vs perceived orientation
key_findings: For neutral current preceded by illusory prior, max serial dependence at matched perceived orientation
For illusory current preceded by neutral prior, max serial dependence at matched physical orientation
interpretations: Priors are high-level constructs incorporating contextual information
Priors interact directly with early sensory signals before flanker-induced bias
